production_blockers: Copernicus DEM tiles are downloaded but not clipped, reprojected, hydrologically conditioned, or imported as Unreal heightfields.
NASA GIBS imagery is coarse and partly cloudy; production Pacuare art still needs higher-resolution cloud-screened Sentinel/Landsat/first-party imagery or licensed aerial references.
Authoritative Costa Rica hydrography, rainfall, discharge, access, and protected-area layers are still under source-rights review; the attached preview centerline is only an editor scaffold.
No Pacuare field/social reference media has explicit game-use rights attached yet.
Rain-fed flow bands remain relative until gauge/rainfall sources and guide feedback are reviewed.

Pacuare rainfall station review now identifies IMN precipitation and SENARA station candidates, but station IDs, features, time series, basin relation, and guide/outfitter validation remain open before numeric rain-fed flow bands or flash-response visuals can be promoted.

Pacuare flash-response review now links rainfall, discharge/stage, basin, and recharge metadata leads, but rainfall-to-stage lag, station time series, basin relation, unsafe-flow criteria, rescue readability, evacuation constraints, and guide/outfitter validation remain open before flash scenarios or high-water visuals can be promoted.

Pacuare River reference annotations and rights manifest are attached as link-only review scaffolds, but no item-level media rights, first-party capture, station geometry, or guide approval is recorded yet.

Pacuare cloud-screened scene index and cloud/shadow review are attached for retained MODIS/GIBS candidates, but production imagery still needs higher-resolution Sentinel/Landsat/local orthophoto or first-party/permissioned aerial sources.

Pacuare consolidated source metadata review is attached, but it only records local review-gated metadata and does not promote DEM, imagery, hydrography, hydrology, access, protected-area, or media sources to production use.

Pacuare high-resolution scene metadata review identifies official Landsat/Sentinel candidates, but production imagery still needs full-year paging, download approval, manual visual QA, CRS/band/scale review, and guide/geospatial signoff before replacing the MODIS/GIBS preview.

Anonymous LandsatLook product asset URLs returned EROS login HTML for the best Pacuare Landsat candidate, so imagery downloads remain blocked until an approved access path returns expected JPEG/JSON/COG content.

Pacuare Sentinel COG thumbnails and tile metadata are attached as visual triage, but full-resolution Sentinel bands, SCL/QA masks, corridor clipping, terms/attribution, and manual cloud/shadow QA are still required before source-drape or mask replacement.
